Description: Drop in systolic blood pressure (>= 20mmHg) or MAP (>=10) during HD;- Diziness, lightheadedness, blurred vision, nausea, or muscle cramps.
ImmediateIntervention: Place patient in Trendelenburg position; Give saline bolus; Stop UF; Provide Oxygen if needed.

Description: Involuntary muscle contractions, often associated with rapid ultrafiltration or low dialysate sodium/calcium
ImmediateIntervention: Reduce UF rate; Administer NS bolus(100-200ml); Stretching of the affected muscle.

Description: Sudden shortness of breath, chest pain, and "doom" feeling;- Life-threatening entry of air into the venous line
ImmediateIntervention: The DURANT Maneuver;- Clamp the lines, Stop the pump, Positioning(The Durant Position - Left side in Trendelenberg i.e head down, feet up); 100% high flow oxygen via mask; Call for help.

Description: Any discomfort, pressure, or pain in the chest area during dialysis;-  Must be differentiated between cardiac ischemia, air embolism, or anxiety. May radiate to the jaw, neck or left arm.
ImmediateIntervention: Stop pump immediately; Administer Oxygen; Notify Superior; Perform ECG; Consider sublingual nitroglycerine

Description: Usually mild, due to fluid shifts
ImmediateIntervention: Check blood pressure; Administer Paracetamol; Dim the lights, Assess for other neurological symptoms to rule out DDS.

Description: Bleeding from fistula, graft, or catheter
ImmediateIntervention: Apply firm manual pressure to site; If from catheter, check caps/clamps; If from fistula, use sterile gauze and pressure.

Description: Rapid drop in blood Glucose < 3.9mmol/L  characterized by sudden cold sweating(diaphoresis), tremors, palpitations and neuroglycopenic signs like confusion, irritability, or LOC.
ImmediateIntervention: Check blood glucose; Administer IV Dextrose 25g(50ml) 0f 50%. Re-check glucose in 15mins.

Description: Headache, nausea, blurred vision, restlessness, severe cases seizures or coma - Is a neurologic syndrome caused by rapid removal of urea from the blood during HD, leadimg to cerebral edema.
ImmediateIntervention: Reduce BFR; Notify Superior; Consider mannitol or hypertonic saline; Stop session if severe.

Description: Sudden onset high fever, rigors, and hypotension 1-2hrs into the session;- Is a systemic inflammatory response due to presence of endotoxins or pyrogens in the dialysate or water system.
ImmediateIntervention: Stop Dialysis immediately; DO NOT return blood; Take blood cultures from blood and dialysate; Administer antipyretics; Check water/dialysate system.

Description: Anaphylactic reaction to the dialyzer membrane/sterilant.
ImmediateIntervention: Stop blood pump; Administer antihistamines/steroids; Do not reuse that dialyzer type.

Description: Irregular heartbeat, often from electrolyte shifts
ImmediateIntervention: Monitor vitals and ECG; Assess for chest pain or shortness of breath; Notify Superior; Check potassium levels in dialysate; Have crash cart nearby.
Stabilize patient basing on symptoms; Notify Superior; Document specific events in notes for later audit.
